title: EIP-7637: Optimize EOA EXTCODEHASH
description: Optimizes EIP-1052 by changing EXTCODEHASH to return 0x for externally owned accounts (EOAs) that have a balance but no code. Currently, such accounts return keccak256("") instead of 0x, creating a confusing distinction that discourages safe use of EXTCODEHASH for contract-existence checks and wastes gas in patterns like Uniswap V2's address lookups.
author: Jame (@ZWJKFLC)
laymanDescription: When checking whether an Ethereum address is a regular wallet or a smart contract, the current answer changes depending on whether the wallet holds ETH. This proposal makes the answer consistent regardless of balance, simplifying contract-existence checks and saving gas for applications like decentralized exchanges.

endUsers.description: No direct user-facing change; contracts that rely on EXTCODEHASH may become cheaper to call, slightly reducing transaction fees.

appDevs.description: Simplifies contract-existence checks; developers can safely use codehash == 0 without worrying about the balance edge case introduced by EIP-1052.

walletDevs.description: No impact on wallet interfaces or transaction construction; the change is internal to EVM opcode semantics.

toolingInfra.description: Debuggers and state inspection tools must update EXTCODEHASH expectations for funded EOAs, reflecting the new return value.

elClients.description: Small change to EXTCODEHASH opcode handler: add a check for the empty-code keccak256 hash and return zero instead. Low implementation complexity.

clClients.description: No consensus-layer changes required; this is purely an execution-layer opcode modification.

benefits: Enables safe, gas-efficient contract-existence checks using codehash == 0.
Removes a confusing EIP-1052 edge case that discourages EXTCODEHASH adoption.
Saves gas for protocols like Uniswap V2 that check address occupancy.
tradeoffs: Breaks contracts that use EXTCODEHASH to detect whether a codeless address holds a balance.

scaleL1.description: Reduces gas cost of on-chain contract-existence checks, improving L1 execution efficiency for high-volume patterns.
